steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content: A $30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle has side lengths in the ratio $1: \sqrt{3}:2$. Let the shortest side be $x$, so the sides are $x$, $x\sqrt{3}$, and $2x$.

steps.title: Match the perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter in terms of $x$ is
$$x+x\sqrt{3}+2x=x(3+\sqrt{3})$$
The given perimeter factors the same way.
$$21+7\sqrt{3}=7(3+\sqrt{3})$$

steps.title: Find the longest side
steps.content:
Thus $x=7$. The longest side is the hypotenuse, $2x$.
$$2x=2(7)=14$$
The answer is 14.
